category_title: Severely disaffected learners with mental health issues and writing barriers
minimal_context: The student is working on a qualification coursework assignment in their workbook during a one-to-one support session.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please open your workbook and start writing the answers for the next section of your qualification coursework assignment.

STUDENT: (Arrives looking upset) I don't want to do this today. Writing is always a massive barrier for me.

TUTOR: Just try to get as much written down as you can.

STUDENT: (Writes a few sentences and stops) I've messed it up already. It's full of mistakes and crossed-out words.

TUTOR: Since there are mistakes, you need to copy it again on the next page so it's readable.

STUDENT: I'm not copying it again! I have lots of writing to do and it's already full of mistakes. It's just too disheartening to copy it all over again.

options.A: Complete consulting notes on a daily basis to share information and strategies with other staff members.
options.B: Conduct functional analyses in the one-to-one support setting where the behavior naturally occurs and the intervention is applied.
options.C: Contact the student's mother and other teachers, and investigate school records to better understand the student.
options.D: Spend the first term developing trust and building a caring and stable relationship with the student.
